Off Calendar Jun-08-2022 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ryutaro Hirota appeared by zoom for Yoko Yamaguchi and Jane Cheng.

The Court stated that a Parent can't waive bond for a child unless she is appointed as guardian of the estate, or guardian ad litem. Mr. Hirota that William Hong is not a direct beneficiary, that he is an alternative beneficiary in the event the Mother wasn't present to take. The Court noted that the codicil isn't very clear, and that William is one and lives in Thailand. Mr. Hirota stated that he will look into his Client obtaining a bond for the full amount of $3,245,000.00, that most of the assets are real property, that there's not a whole lot of liquid assets and that the plan is to distribute 45% to Ms. Yamaguchi, 45% to William's Mother and 10% to June. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated May 14, 2019 and the codicil dated June 8, 2019 are admitted to probate. Yoko Yamaguchi and Jane Hom Cheng are appointed as the Executors of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $3,245,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for June 8, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.
